title: paeonia wittmanniana subsp. tomentosa
intro: Paeonia wittmanniana subsp. tomentosa, also known as the woolly peony, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant native to China. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Paeonia wittmanniana subsp. tomentosa include reducing pain and inflammation, treating fever and headaches, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
